conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately, overall?
Tired. It feels like I am dragging myself through the day. I just want to get home and rest.
How long has it felt like you're dragging through the day?
A few months, I guess. It started around the time the kids went back to school. I just didn't have the energy to keep up with the pace anymore.
Is it harder to focus or concentrate than it used to be?
Yeah, it's like my brain is foggy. I'll be sitting at my desk, and I'll just stare at the screen because I can't think of what to do next.
How long does that foggy feeling usually last when you're at your desk?
It depends. Sometimes I get a few emails done and I'm good to go. Other times, I sit there for hours and get nothing done. It's just hit or miss.
How have you been sleeping lately?
Not great. I wake up in the middle of the night and can't get back to sleep. Or I'll fall asleep at 10 pm and wake up at 2 am. It's been weird.
How long has this been happening?
A few weeks, I think. It started when my wife started working the night shift. I just feel like I'm not getting any rest.
Been doing anything you enjoy lately?
Not really. I used to play football with my mates, but I haven't been able to keep up with them for a while. Now I just watch TV or sit around.
What's kept you from playing football with your mates?
I just can't run anymore. I'm 40, I guess. I used to be able to keep up, but now I'm just exhausted after a 30-minute game.
When you think about the next few weeks, what do you expect day to day?
More of the same. Just trying to get through each day without losing my mind. I'm just waiting for the weekend, to be honest.
What does getting through the day look like for you right now?
It looks like a struggle. I get up, go to work, and then go home. I don't really do much. I just exist.
When you say that, is it more about wanting to withdraw from everything, or feeling like you don't want to be here?
It's a bit of both, to be honest. I feel like I'm just going through the motions. But I don't have the energy to figure out how to change it, so I just keep doing what I'm doing.
When you say that, are you having thoughts about ending your life?
No, no. It's just the stress of it all. I'm just tired and frustrated. I wouldn't really do anything drastic. I'm just stuck.
Do you find it harder to make decisions than before, even small ones?
Yeah. Everything feels like a chore. I look at the laundry and think, 'Why bother?' It's just easier to ignore it.
How long has it felt easier to just ignore things like the laundry?
I don't know, a few months maybe? It just feels like I'm carrying a heavy weight, and doing the laundry is just too much for me right now.
Do you feel like you have enough energy for your usual day?
No. I feel like I'm running on empty. Even little things, like taking the kids to school, feels like a massive task.
How long has taking the kids to school felt like such a big task?
A few weeks, I think. It used to be easy. Now I just feel like I'm dragging myself there.
Are you still into the things you used to care about?
Not really. I used to love playing football, but I can't run anymore. I just don't have the energy for hobbies.
What's it like when you try to start something you used to enjoy?
It feels like a chore. I look at the TV remote and think, 'I don't have the energy for this either.' I just sit there.
Is there anything weighing on your mind that you wish you could change?
Just the fact that I'm 40 and I feel like I've lost my way. I wish I could just turn back the clock and be the person I used to be.
What do you miss most about the person you used to be?
The energy. I used to be able to do stuff, to keep up. Now I just feel like I'm running on empty all the time.
How do you feel about how things have gone for you recently?
I don't know. It's just... I thought I was supposed to be further along by now. I guess I'm just stuck.
What does being "further along" look like to you?
I don't know. I guess it means having a career, being in a better place financially... I don't know what I'm doing with my life anymore.
How's your appetite been?
Bad. I'm always tired, so I don't eat much. I just grab something quick and go back to bed.
How long has the tiredness been affecting your eating like this?
A few months. It's just been a constant struggle.
Have you found yourself getting emotional more easily lately?
Yeah. I get frustrated with the kids for no reason. I just feel like I'm on edge all the time.
